𝗠𝗖𝗣-𝗦𝗦𝗛-𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗔𝗚𝗘𝗥 | Enabling AI models to securely and efficiently manage and automate tasks on remote SSH servers.  📈 Growing!

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Enabling AI models to securely and efficiently manage and automate tasks on remote SSH servers.
📅 Created: Aug 2025 • Updated: Apr 2026

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Remote SSH server management via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
✅ Compatibility with Claude Code and OpenAI Codex
✅ Execute commands on remote servers
✅ Transfer files to/from remote servers
✅ Database operations
✅ DevOps automation
✅ Health monitoring
✅ SSH ProxyJump/Bastion Host support
✅ SSH Agent and passphrase support
✅ Windows SSH host compatibility


┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 124 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 23 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 4 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

MCP SSH Manager is a Node.js server that allows AI models like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex to manage remote SSH connections. It facilitates executing commands, transferring files, and automating DevOps tasks on multiple servers through the Model Context Protocol.

┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉┉

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/bvisible/mcp-ssh-manager

𝗧𝗘𝗥𝗠𝗜𝗡𝗔𝗟-𝗨𝗦𝗘 | To provide AI agents with a functional, headless terminal environment to interact with command-line applications, enabling complex tasks like navigating file systems, running text-based games, or interacting with ncurses UIs.  🔥 Super Hot!

╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide AI agents with a functional, headless terminal environment to interact with command-line applications, enabling complex tasks like navigating file systems, running text-based games, or interacting with ncurses UIs.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Headless virtual terminal for AI agents
✅ Spawn interactive terminal apps
✅ Read terminal screen output
✅ Send keystrokes to terminal applications
✅ No GUI, X server, or display needed
✅ Real-time monitoring of agent activity
✅ Daemon manages sessions automatically
✅ Configurable terminal size and TERM environment variable


╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

terminal-use (`tu`) is a Rust-based headless virtual terminal emulator designed for AI agents. It allows AI agents to spawn and interact with command-line applications in a simulated terminal environment, enabling them to read screen output and send keystrokes without needing a graphical interface or X server.

╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

𝗢𝗣𝗘𝗡𝗖𝗟𝗔𝗨𝗗𝗘 | To provide developers with a single, consistent command-line interface for interacting with a wide variety of AI coding models, both cloud-based and local, streamlining their development workflow.  🔍 Worth a Look

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide developers with a single, consistent command-line interface for interacting with a wide variety of AI coding models, both cloud-based and local, streamlining their development workflow.
📅 Created: Apr 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Unified CLI for multiple AI model providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama, GitHub Models, Codex, etc.)
✅ Terminal-first workflow with prompts, tools, agents, MCP, and slash commands
✅ Support for OpenAI-compatible APIs, local servers, and Apple Silicon backends
✅ Streaming output for real-time responses and tool progress
✅ Tool-driven coding workflows including bash, file operations, grep, glob, and web tools
✅ Provider profile management for easy setup and switching


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 12.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 4.4K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 47 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

OpenClaude is an open-source, terminal-first coding agent CLI that unifies access to over 200 AI models, including OpenAI, Gemini, and local options like Ollama. It provides a consistent workflow for developers to interact with various AI backends for coding tasks, leveraging features like slash commands, agents, and streaming output.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Neko is a self-hosted virtual browser running in Docker, utilizing WebRTC for secure and private internet access from anywhere. It allows multiple users to access a shared browser environment simultaneously, making it suitable for collaboration, remote work, and shared viewing experiences like watch parties and interactive presentations.

𝗩𝗦𝗥𝗙𝗜-𝗖𝗢𝗠𝗙𝗬𝗨𝗜 | To upscale and enhance the frame rate of long videos within the ComfyUI environment, overcoming memory limitations.  🌟 Rising Fast!

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To upscale and enhance the frame rate of long videos within the ComfyUI environment, overcoming memory limitations.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Video Super Resolution using Flash VSR
✅ Frame Interpolation with GIMM-VFI, RIFE, or FILM
✅ Smart Tile Processing for VRAM optimization
✅ Stream Processing for arbitrary video lengths
✅ Automatic model downloads


✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

⭐⭐ 55 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 6 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This ComfyUI custom node enhances video processing by combining Video Super Resolution (VSR) with Frame Interpolation (VFI). It supports arbitrary video lengths through smart tiling and stream processing, allowing for memory-efficient upscaling and frame rate doubling.

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦
